texto: When people speak of great men, they think of men like Napoleon - men of violence. Rarely do they think of peaceful men. But contrast the reception they will receive when they return home from their battles. Napoleon will arrive in pomp and in power, a man who's achieved the very summit of earthly ambition. And yet his dreams will be haunted by the oppressions of war. William Wilberforce, however, will return to his family, lay his head on his pillow and remember: the slave trade is no more.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charles Fox: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “When people speak of great men, they think of men like Napoleon - men of violence. Rarely do they…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, inner-peace, self-esteem pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de self-esteem.

frase_original: When people speak of great men, they think of men like Napoleon - men of violence. Rarely do they think of peaceful men. But contrast the reception they will receive when they return home from their battles. Napoleon will arrive in pomp and in power, a man who's achieved the very summit of earthly ambition. And yet his dreams will be haunted by the oppressions of war. William Wilberforce, however, will return to his family, lay his head on his pillow and remember: the slave trade is no more.
